message:
feat(parser,render): add kanban metadata (@{wip,priority,assigned}), priority colors, and clippy fixes

Kanban parser: extract @{wip: N, priority: high, assigned: user}
metadata from column headers and cards. WIP limits set cluster grid
span; priority and assigned fields emit CSS classes for downstream
styling. Deduplicate cursor advance in entity decoder.

SVG renderer: map kanban-priority-{high,critical,medium,low} classes
to fill colors (red/yellow/green) for visual card prioritization.

Parser tests: replace direct f32 equality with epsilon comparisons
to satisfy clippy float_cmp lint; suppress unreadable_literal on
LCG constants in property-based tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-508m","title":"Implement sequence diagram autonumber","description":"# Sequence Autonumber

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  Alice->>Bob: First (1)
  Bob->>Alice: Second (2)
\`\`\`
- \`autonumber\` enables sequential numbering on all messages
- Numbers appear as a small circled number next to each message arrow
- Can be toggled on/off mid-diagram

## Current State
- NOT parsed. \`autonumber\` line produces a warning.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Recognize \`autonumber\` as SequenceStatement::Autonumber.
2. **IR**: Add autonumber: bool flag to IrSequenceMeta.
3. **SVG Renderer**: When autonumber is true, prepend a circled number to each message label.
4. **Tests**: Autonumber on, verify numbers appear in SVG.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.887912141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:02:25.665996068Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:02:25.665460955Z","close_reason":"Implemented autonumber parsing and SVG rendering. Parser recognizes 'autonumber' keyword as SequenceStatement::Autonumber, sets IrSequenceMeta.autonumber=true via IrBuilder.enable_autonumber(). SVG renderer prepends edge number to labels when autonumber is enabled. 3 parser tests added. - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)

 {"id":"bd-54qp","title":"Pie: Implement title rendering and custom slice colors","description":"# Pie Chart Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Title (\`pie title "Title"\`) is parsed but not rendered in SVG output
- Custom color per slice not supported (uses theme defaults)
- showData flag parsed but value display not implemented
- Legend box not rendered

## Implementation Plan
1. Render pie title as centered text above the chart
2. When showData is true, show percentage and value next to each slice label

 {"id":"bd-5slz","title":"Replace hardcoded diagram title color in scene renderer","description":"## What
Replace hardcoded dark gray (#1f2937) for diagram titles in the scene-based render path (render_scene_document_with_ir) with a theme-aware value.

## Why
The scene-based renderer at fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs line ~341 uses `.fill("#1f2937")` for diagram titles. This dark color is invisible on Dark, Blueprint, and Neon themes.

## Technical Context
This is in `render_scene_document_with_ir()` where the title is rendered BEFORE `let theme = resolve_theme()` at line ~361. The theme is not yet available when the title is rendered.

## Options
1. **Move title rendering after theme resolution** — requires restructuring the function, since the title needs to be in the SVG before the main content for z-ordering
2. **Use CSS custom property** — `.fill("var(--fm-text-color, #1f2937)")` — works when theme CSS is embedded
3. **Pre-resolve theme for title** — call `resolve_theme()` earlier just for the title color

Option 2 is the simplest and most robust since the theme CSS block IS embedded before the title element.

## Specific Changes
- fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs line ~341: Change `.fill("#1f2937")` to `.fill("var(--fm-text-color, #1f2937)")`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Diagram titles are readable on all 10 theme presets
- No visual regression on Default theme (fallback is the same dark gray)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:32.780097774Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:13.503408799Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:13.502905336Z","close_reason":"Title now uses var(--fm-text-color, #1f2937) CSS custom property.  {"id":"bd-bfsm","title":"Implement classDef directive parsing and application","description":"# classDef Directive Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
classDef defines reusable CSS-like style classes that can be applied to nodes:
\`\`\`
classDef important fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px
classDef default fill:#fff
class nodeA,nodeB important
\`\`\`

Supported style properties: fill, stroke, stroke-width, stroke-dasharray, color, font-size, font-weight, font-family, background, border-radius, padding.

## Current State in frankenmermaid
- The \`class nodeA className\` syntax IS parsed (mermaid_parser.rs) and classes are stored on IrNode.classes
- The \`classDef\` syntax itself is NOT parsed — no style definitions are captured
- The SVG renderer does not apply any class-based styling to nodes

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser** (mermaid_parser.rs): Parse \`classDef className prop:value,prop:value\` lines. Store in a new \`IrStyleDef\` type in MermaidDiagramIr (add to fm-core).
2. **IR** (fm-core/src/lib.rs): Add \`style_defs: BTreeMap<String, IrStyleDef>\` to MermaidDiagramIr. IrStyleDef contains fill, stroke, stroke_width, stroke_dasharray, color, etc.
3. **SVG Renderer** (fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs): When rendering a node, look up its classes in style_defs and apply fill/stroke/etc. as inline SVG attributes.
4. **Terminal Renderer**: No visual impact (terminal doesn't support colors in basic mode).

## Test Plan
- Parse classDef with single property
- Parse classDef with multiple comma-separated properties
- Apply class to node and verify SVG attributes
- Verify default classDef applies to unclassed nodes
- Verify classDef with quoted values","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.353430251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:18:47.961647754Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:18:47.961366301Z","close_reason":"classDef parsing already existed via extract_style_directives; added populate_structured_styles() call in ir_builder::finish() to populate style_defs and per-node/edge inline_style.  {"id":"bd-bium","title":"Implement flowchart parallel edge syntax (A & B --> C & D)","description":"# Flowchart Parallel Edge Syntax

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
flowchart LR
  A & B --> C & D
\`\`\`
This creates 4 edges: A→C, A→D, B→C, B→D. The & operator allows declaring multiple source/target nodes in a single edge statement.

## Current State
- NOT parsed. The & character is not recognized in edge declarations.
- Mermaid parser's edge AST only supports single source → single target.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: In parse_edge_statement_asts(), before looking for arrow operators, split node references on '&'. If either side contains '&', expand to multiple FlowAst::Edge entries (cartesian product of sources × targets).
2. **Tests**: A & B --> C (2 edges), A --> B & C (2 edges), A & B --> C & D (4 edges), single node (no &, unchanged behavior).

## Considerations
- The & operator is only valid in flowchart, not other diagram types
- Must handle whitespace: 'A&B' vs 'A & B' vs 'A &B'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:14.744584229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T01:31:57.010578087Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T01:31:57.010075205Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser"],"comments":[{"id":195,"issue_id":"bd-bium","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

 {"id":"bd-drav","title":"Parse and render sequence diagram interaction fragments (loop/alt/opt/par/critical/break)","description":"# Sequence Interaction Fragments

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  Alice->>Bob: Request
  loop Every minute
    Bob->>Alice: Heartbeat
  end
  alt Success
    Bob->>Alice: 200 OK
  else Failure
    Bob->>Alice: 500 Error
  end
  opt Optional
    Alice->>Carol: Forward
  end
  par Parallel
    Alice->>Bob: Task 1
  and
    Alice->>Carol: Task 2
  end
  critical Critical Section
    Alice->>Bob: Important
  option Timeout
    Alice->>Bob: Retry
  end
  break When error
    Alice->>Bob: Abort
  end
  rect rgb(200, 220, 240)
    Alice->>Bob: Highlighted
  end
\`\`\`

Fragment types: loop, alt/else, opt, par/and, critical/option, break, rect (colored background).
Each fragment is a labeled box spanning the involved participants, enclosing the messages within.
Fragments can be nested.

## Current State
- NOT parsed. Fragment keywords produce warnings.
- No layout support for fragment regions.
- No SVG rendering of fragment boxes.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Add SequenceStatement::FragmentStart { kind, label }, FragmentElse { label }, FragmentEnd. Track nesting depth. Kind enum: Loop, Alt, Opt, Par, Critical, Break, Rect.
2. **IR**: Store as Vec<IrSequenceFragment> with kind, label, start_message_index, end_message_index, participant_range, and nested children.
3. **Layout**: Fragments add padding around their contained messages. Compute fragment box bounds from the first/last message y-positions and the leftmost/rightmost participant x-positions. Nested fragments get progressively larger bounds.
4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw fragment boxes as dashed-border rectangles with a label tab in the top-left corner. Fragment kind shown in bold in the tab. 'else'/'and'/'option' dividers shown as dashed horizontal lines within the fragment.
5. **Tests**: loop, alt/else, opt, par/and, nested fragments, rect with color.

## Complexity
This is the single most complex sequence diagram feature. Fragment nesting requires a stack-based parser and recursive layout. Budget 2-3x the effort of a simple task.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.719718813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:25:34.360084926Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:25:34.359593546Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence interaction fragment parsing (loop, alt, opt, par, critical, break, rect).  {"id":"bd-qiyv","title":"Make quadrant chart dimensions configurable","description":"## What
Replace hardcoded quadrant chart dimensions (chart_w=400, chart_h=400, margin_left=80, margin_top=60) with configurable or content-adaptive values.

## Why
Both the layout (layout_diagram_quadrant_traced) and SVG renderer (render_quadrant_svg) use identical hardcoded 400x400 chart dimensions. This means:
- The chart does not adapt to the available viewport/terminal size
- Long axis labels can overflow the margins
- The chart is always square even if the content suggests a different aspect ratio
- Terminal rendering at small sizes wastes most of the 400x400 area

## Proposed Approach
1. Add `chart_width` and `chart_height` fields to a `QuadrantLayoutConfig` (or reuse LayoutSpacing)
2. Compute margins from axis label dimensions using FontMetrics
3. In terminal renderer, scale to the actual cell dimensions instead of using fixed 400x400
4. Keep 400x400 as the default for backward compatibility

## Acceptance Criteria
- Quadrant charts render correctly at different terminal sizes
- Long axis labels do not overflow
- Default behavior unchanged for typical usage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:10.923449900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:39:08.407968687Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:39:08.407452109Z","close_reason":"Quadrant chart_w/chart_h now scale with point count: base=300+15*n, clamped [200,600].  {"id":"bd-r5y9","title":"Extract sequence layout magic numbers into LayoutSpacing","description":"## What
Move hardcoded sequence-specific spacing constants from layout_diagram_sequence_traced() into LayoutSpacing or a new SequenceLayoutConfig struct.

## Why
The sequence layout function contains several magic numbers that control diagram appearance:
- `participant_gap = spacing.node_spacing + 80.0` — the +80.0 offset is hardcoded
- `message_gap = spacing.rank_spacing.max(56.0)` — the 56.0 minimum is hardcoded
- Self-loop dimensions: `loop_width = 40.0`, `loop_height = message_gap * 0.6`
- Fragment padding: `padding = 20.0` (should use LayoutSpacing.cluster_padding)
- Fragment total_width fallback: `200.0`
- Activation bar width: `10.0` (in build_activation_bars)

These should be configurable so users can tune sequence diagram density/compactness.

## Proposed Approach
1. Add to LayoutSpacing: `sequence_participant_gap_extra: f32` (default 80.0), `sequence_min_message_gap: f32` (default 56.0), `sequence_self_loop_width: f32` (default 40.0), `sequence_activation_width: f32` (default 10.0)
2. Use `spacing.cluster_padding` for fragment padding instead of hardcoded 20.0
3. Keep defaults equal to current hardcoded values for backward compatibility

## Acceptance Criteria
- All sequence spacing configurable through LayoutSpacing
- Default behavior unchanged
- Users can create compact sequences by reducing spacing values","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:40.258232874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:36:32.499563562Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:36:32.499073385Z","close_reason":"Added sequence_participant_gap_extra, sequence_min_message_gap, sequence_self_loop_width, sequence_activation_width to LayoutSpacing.
